Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone export from roots and likely shootward/local transport; loss reduces orobanchol exudation, impairs AM fungal interaction, and increases branching.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root exudates/rhizosphere; shoot branching context in aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling to arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and inhibition of shoot branching
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reverse genetics of pdr1 mutant; expression analysis; subcellular localization; root exudate LC-MS/MS; isotope-labeled GR24 transport assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDR1 is required for efficient strigolactone export from roots into the rhizosphere. 2. pdr1 mutants show reduced orobanchol in root exudates and reduced AM fungal colonization. 3. PDR1 is asymmetrically localized, supporting dual roles in shootward transport from root tip and export from hypodermal passage cells. 4. pdr1 mutants also show hyper-branching, implicating a role in aerial strigolactone transport.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed at hypodermal passage cells in roots; also at vasculature and nodes near leaf axes; induced by phosphate starvation and AM colonization
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Apical membrane of hypodermal cells in root tip; outer lateral membrane of hypodermal passage cells
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces mycorrhizal interaction and causes hyper-branching
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate rhizosphere SL exudation, AM symbiosis, and plant architecture, but substrate specificity remains unresolved
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Actual transported substrate is not definitively established; orobanchol is a candidate but PDR1 may transport multiple SL-related compounds.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone export associated with mycorrhization; mutants reduce AM fungal colonization.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Symbiotic signaling for AM fungi and nutrient acquisition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based functional analysis; expression profiling; mutant analysis for mycorrhizal colonization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtABCG59 is a PDR1 homolog implicated in root strigolactone export. 2. Expression is elevated under phosphate limitation. 3. Defective mutants show reduced AM fungal colonization, consistent with impaired SL-related signaling/export.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in root tip and cortical cells; induced under phosphate-limiting conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering mycorrhization efficiency in legumes via SL export control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: M. truncatula lacks hypodermal passage cells, indicating anatomical variation in transporter deployment.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes strigolactone transport in aerial tissues to inhibit branching; knockdown increases secondary stems with lateral branches.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Aerial transport context near stem nodes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtPDR6 is a close PDR1 homolog implicated in strigolactone transport. 2. Knockdown lines exhibit increased branching. 3. Expression in lower stem nodes supports a role in transporting branching-inhibitory SL signals.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Observed in nodes at lower parts of the stem

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the principal strigolactone receptor mediating branching inhibition and signaling; also hydrolyzes SL analogs.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Control of shoot branching and other SL responses
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant characterization; crystal structure; ligand hydrolysis assays; differential scanning fluorimetry; complementation; protein interaction assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. d14 is an SL-insensitive mutant identifying D14 as a central SL signaling component. 2. D14-family proteins bind/hydrolyze SL analogs and interact with signaling partners in an SL-dependent manner. 3. Competing models propose hydrolysis-dependent or hydrolysis-independent activation, but D14 is widely accepted as the receptor. 4. Catalytically inactive AtD14D218A can complement atd14, supporting hydrolysis-independent signal initiation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central target for manipulating SL responsiveness and architecture
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This review emphasizes mechanistic debate over whether intact SL or a hydrolysis intermediate triggers the active signaling state.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses strigolactone signaling; degradation relieves repression and enables branching inhibition.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant characterization; protein interaction; proteasome-dependent degradation assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D53 is a repressor of strigolactone signaling. 2. In the presence of SL, D53 interacts with D14 and is degraded via a D3-dependent 26S proteasome pathway. 3. Dominant d53 mutant protein constitutively suppresses SL signaling, causing insensitivity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful node for tuning SL sensitivity downstream of hormone production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous repressors in Arabidopsis are SMXL6, SMXL7, and SMXL8.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa; Pisum sativum
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa; Pisum sativum
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive signaling component for strigolactone perception; required for degradation of signaling repressors and for feedback control of biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulation of shoot branching and SL responses
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; genetic comparison across species; protein interaction and degradation studies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAX2/D3/RMS4 defines a conserved class of SL-insensitive mutants. 2. The proteins are orthologous F-box factors required for ubiquitination-dependent signaling. 3. D3 cooperates with D14 to promote degradation of D53-family repressors.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core node for altering overall SL sensitivity, though likely pleiotropic
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initially considered possible direct receptors, but now placed downstream/in complex with D14.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes all-trans-β-carotene to 9-cis-β-carotene as the first committed carotenoid-derived step toward strigolactones.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SL biosynthesis; loss causes SL deficiency and increased branching/tillering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; recombinant enzyme pathway reconstruction with CCD7 and CCD8; LC-MS/MS; feeding studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D27 acts upstream of CCD7 and CCD8 in the core SL pathway. 2. Together with CCD7 and CCD8, it enables production of carlactone from all-trans-β-carotene. 3. D27 expression is induced by phosphate starvation and suppressed by cytokinin, gibberellin, and zaxinone in rice.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Regulated in roots by phosphorus status and hormonal cues
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point not firmly established; core upstream enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for manipulating total SL output and architecture/symbiosis responses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NSP1/NSP2 positively regulate D27 transcription under phosphate deficiency in several species.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-β-carotene to generate 9-cis-β-apo-10'-carotenal for downstream conversion to carlactone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SL production; mutants are SL deficient and highly branched/high tillering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis across species; in vitro recombinant enzyme assays with D27 and CCD8
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD7 is a conserved core SL biosynthetic enzyme identified genetically from branching mutants. 2. It acts with D27 and CCD8 to produce carlactone from β-carotene. 3. Its transcript is feedback-regulated by SL signaling and responsive to auxin and nutrient/hormone status.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Regulated by auxin; increased in SL-deficient/insensitive backgrounds; nutrient-responsive in rice roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin; phosphate deficiency; cytokinin; gibberellin; zaxinone; GR24 feedback
Pathway_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback by SL signaling inferred from transcript upregulation in SL-deficient/insensitive mutants and reversal by GR24
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots/root exudates and shoot branching system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A conserved target of SL negative feedback regulation.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa; Petunia hybrida
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa; Petunia hybrida
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 9-cis-β-apo-10'-carotenal into carlactone via oxidative rearrangement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SL biosynthesis; mutants are SL deficient and show increased branching/tillering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; in vitro recombinant pathway assays; LC-MS/MS detection of intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD8 is a conserved core biosynthetic enzyme required for carlactone formation. 2. The CCD8-catalyzed reaction yields (11R)-carlactone, considered critical for natural SL stereochemistry. 3. CCD8 transcripts are under negative feedback and regulated by auxin, nutrient status, cytokinin, gibberellin, and zaxinone.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots/root tip contexts; cortex cells in petunia root tip; transcriptionally responsive to hormones and nutrient availability
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; major conserved control node by feedback
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin; phosphate deficiency; cytokinin; gibberellin; zaxinone; GR24 feedback
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots/root exudates and shoot branching system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CL downstream mobility inferred from grafting; DAD1/CCD8 expression in root cortex informed PDR1 transport model.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C-19 methyl of carlactone through three sequential oxidations to produce carlactonoic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms central intermediate for downstream SL products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Strigolactones; carlactonoic acid-derived products; methyl carlactonoate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes downstream SL formation; max1 mutants accumulate carlactone and show hyper-branching.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; grafting; recombinant yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis MAX1 acts downstream of CCD8 and converts carlactone to carlactonoic acid. 2. max1 mutants overaccumulate carlactone, confirming substrate assignment. 3. MAX1 prefers (11R)-carlactone, contributing to efficient formation of natural-configured SLs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during leaf senescence in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MAX1 transcripts were also reported in xylem-sap and mobility discussions indirectly via grafting.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes canonical SL biosynthesis in rice roots and exudates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays; metabolite profiling; transcription-metabolite correlation under nutrient conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os900 converts carlactone toward canonical rice SL production, yielding 4-deoxyorobanchol. 2. Its expression correlates with endogenous 4DO levels under phosphorus availability changes. 3. It is downregulated by cytokinin, gibberellin, and zaxinone under relevant conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP711A conversion of CL to CLA is suggested to be broadly conserved, but rice Os900 specifically supports 4DO formation.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os1400 catalyzes the final hydroxylation step from 4DO to orobanchol in rice. 2. Expression is responsive to nutrient and hormone cues similarly to other rice SL biosynthetic genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Representative CYP711A3-type reaction is not universal across plants.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Shown to catalyze an A2-type CYP711A reaction analogous to CL-to-4DO conversion.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Contributes to canonical SL branch formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports conserved diversification of SL biosynthesis outside flowering plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical enzyme characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmCYP711A17v1/v3 can catalyze a CYP711A2-type reaction. 2. This supports evolutionary conservation/diversification of CYP711A-mediated canonical SL biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as comparative evidence in review rather than a focal experimental subject of a single study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Shown to catalyze a CYP711A3-type hydroxylation reaction analogous to 4DO-to-ORO conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports diversification of canonical SL biosynthesis in maize.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical enzyme characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCYP711A18 catalyzes a CYP711A3-type reaction in SL biosynthesis. 2. This indicates that specialized CYP711A activities evolved in multiple plant lineages.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts carlactonoic acid to orobanchol, probably via 18-hydroxy-carlactonoic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VuCYP722C converts CLA to orobanchol. 2. The reaction in vitro is not stereospecific, yielding ent-2'-epi-orobanchol in addition to orobanchol. 3. Additional factors may be required in vivo for stereocontrol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes possible requirement for other stereochemistry-determining factor(s).

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts carlactonoic acid to orobanchol likely via 18-hydroxy-CLA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for ORO-type SL production in tomato, but not apparently for branching inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; mutant analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCYP722C is required for production of orobanchol and solanacol in tomato. 2. Slcyp722c mutants fail to produce these ORO-type SLs. 3. Despite this metabolic defect, mutants do not display the increased branching seen in Slccd8, implying ORO-type SLs may not be the branch-inhibiting hormone in tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering this gene may change rhizosphere signaling without strongly affecting branching
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reduce parasitic/symbiotic signaling through canonical SLs while preserving architecture
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Illustrates divergence between rhizosphere SL composition and hormone controlling branching.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion from carlactonoic acid to 5-deoxystrigol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigol-type canonical SL biosynthesis in cotton.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GaCYP722C converts CLA to 5-deoxystrigol. 2. This establishes CYP722C as a determinant of canonical SL structural diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrasts with ORO-forming CYP722Cs in other species.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to function in 5-deoxystrigol biosynthesis downstream of CYP711A9/LjMAX1.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely directly forms canonical SL branch product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes 5DS biosynthesis in Lotus japonicus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic and biochemical evidence summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjCYP722C is proposed to function downstream of LjMAX1 in 5DS biosynthesis. 2. Its precise substrate remains unresolved.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Zea mays; Sorghum bicolor; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methyl carlactonoate to hydroxymethyl-carlactonoate (1'-OH-MeCLA), with CLA also detected prominently during incubation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for formation of active late non-canonical SL-related hormone(s)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of late branch-inhibiting SL-related metabolite(s); lbo mutants accumulate MeCLA yet show mild hyper-branching.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; enzyme assays; metabolite identification; cross-species homolog assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBO acts downstream of MeCLA in late non-canonical SL biosynthesis. 2. lbo mutants accumulate MeCLA but remain hyper-branched, implying the true active product is downstream of MeCLA. 3. The product was identified as 1'-OH-MeCLA, although CLA is often a major observed incubation product. 4. Homologs in maize, sorghum, and tomato catalyze the same reaction.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete understanding of exact bioactive product complicates engineering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for identifying or engineering the active branching hormone form
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main biochemical function may be hydroxylation of MeCLA or effective demethylation through instability of 1'-OH-MeCLA; unresolved.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Unknown; probably MeCLA-, 1'-OH-MeCLA-, or related derivative
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required for lotuslactone biosynthesis, probably acting downstream of MeCLA or 1'-OH-MeCLA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for non-canonical target-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for lotuslactone production in Lotus japonicus.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LLD is a newly identified 2OGD required for lotuslactone biosynthesis. 2. lld mutants fail to produce lotuslactone. 3. The enzyme likely functions downstream of MeCLA or 1'-OH-MeCLA, but exact substrate remains unknown.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or is required for phosphate-starvation-induced expression of D27 and other SL biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates SL production under phosphate starvation; loss reduces SL biosynthetic gene expression, SL output, and AM fungal infection.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray/gene expression analysis; knockout/double knockdown; metabolite profiling; mycorrhization phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSP1 is required for phosphate-deficiency-induced expression of MtD27 and proper SL production in Medicago roots. 2. nsp1 mutants show reduced SL production and reduced AM fungal infection. 3. The conserved role extends to rice, where nsp1 nsp2 double knockdown lowers D27 transcripts and decreases 4DO and ORO in root exudates.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking phosphate starvation to increased SL biosynthesis for symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance low-phosphate symbiotic signaling, though broader symbiosis roles may cause pleiotropy

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for activation/upregulation of D27 and other SL biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate-starvation-induced SL biosynthesis and AM symbiosis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray/gene expression analysis; knockout/double knockdown; metabolite profiling; AM infection phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSP2 functions with NSP1 to drive phosphate-starvation induction of SL biosynthesis genes. 2. Loss of NSP2 reduces MtD27 expression and SL production in Medicago roots. 3. Rice nsp1 nsp2 double knockdown similarly reduces D27 transcripts and 4DO/ORO exudation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: D14-LIKE signaling in rice acts upstream of NSP2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of phosphate-responsive SL increase for AM symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A central integrator of phosphate starvation and symbiotic signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive indirect regulation of SL biosynthetic gene expression via auxin signaling repression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively regulates expression of SlNSP1, SlD27 and SlMAX1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes expression of SL biosynthetic genes in mycorrhized and non-mycorrhized tomato roots.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlIAA27 is induced by mycorrhization. 2. It positively regulates SlNSP1, SlD27 and SlMAX1 expression in roots. 3. This links auxin signaling modulation to SL biosynthesis during symbiotic interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as a positive regulator in tomato roots despite AUX/IAA proteins classically repressing auxin signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Upstream regulation of strigolactone biosynthesis associated with AM symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Induces NSP2 expression, thereby promoting SL synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates SL biosynthesis in rice and is indispensable for AM symbiosis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic pathway analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The rice D14-LIKE pathway positively regulates SL biosynthesis. 2. It acts upstream of NSP2, whose expression is induced by this pathway. 3. The pathway is indispensable for AM symbiosis.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Likely endogenous D14-LIKE ligand(s); exact signal not specified
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream controller connecting symbiosis signaling to SL biosynthetic activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paralogous to canonical SL signaling pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of SL biosynthetic genes downstream of GA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GA signaling suppresses SL biosynthesis and lowers 4DO/ORO levels in rice roots.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hormone application; metabolite profiling; gene expression analysis; genetic analysis of GA-deficient Tan-ginbozu
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bioactive GA strongly suppresses SL biosynthesis in rice roots. 2. The GID1-DELLA pathway mediates repression of major SL biosynthetic genes. 3. GA-deficient plants accumulate higher 4DO and exhibit altered GR24 responsiveness.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong negative hormonal controller of rice canonical SL production
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating this module will broadly affect GA traits beyond SL metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis transcriptome data also support GA-mediated repression of MAX4 and LBO.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Striga hermonthica; transgenic Arabidopsis assay system
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand perception leading to seed germination signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers extremely sensitive SL perception for parasitic seed germination.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic Arabidopsis germination assays; comparative receptor characterization; chemical agonist studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShHTL7 is an SL receptor in the parasitic plant Striga hermonthica. 2. It shows exceptionally high sensitivity to SL relative to other ShHTLs. 3. Non-hydrolyzable agonists can activate signaling, supporting hydrolysis-independent initiation at least in part.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major receptor determining ultra-sensitive germination response to host SLs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for designing suicidal germination agents against Striga
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in review perception section; relevant to SL biology though not plant nutrient metabolite engineering in crops per se.
